requirement: The system shall formulate hypotheses, critically evaluate them, characterize uncertainty, and use that uncertainty to acquire and refine scientific knowledge bases that human scientists can trust.
exact_text_quote: An AI agent should be able to formulate biomedical hypotheses, critically evaluate them, characterize their uncertainty, and use that as a driver to acquire and refine its scientific knowledge bases in a way that human scientists can trust.

requirement: The system shall adapt to new scientific insights by incorporating the latest findings and refining hypotheses based on experimental results.
exact_text_quote: AI agents should be designed to adapt to new biological insights, incorporate the latest scientific findings, and refine hypotheses based on experimental results.

requirement: The system shall maintain conversational history with scientists using natural language so that it can keep track of scientific interactions with agents.
exact_text_quote: We envision that agents will maintain a history of interaction with scientists using natural language, which, in turn, will allow us to keep track of scientific interactions with agents.

requirement: The system shall provide interaction capabilities that allow scientists to express intentions in natural language without needing to search for how and where to accomplish tasks.
exact_text_quote: This approach allows scientists to express their intentions in natural language without needing to search for how and where to accomplish tasks.

requirement: The system shall use memory modules to store and recall information needed for executing complex tasks and adapting to new or evolving environments.
exact_text_quote: Memory modules are designed to store long-term and short-term learned knowledge.

requirement: The system shall update memory with new information as agents encounter new situations and acquire data.
exact_text_quote: As agents encounter new situations and acquire data, memory modules get updated with new information.

requirement: The system shall use reasoning modules to process information and execute action plans.
exact_text_quote: Finally, the reasoning module processes information and executes action plans.

requirement: The system shall support reasoning with feedback by adjusting its plan in response to experimental or human feedback.
exact_text_quote: We classify reasoning modules into two categories: direct reasoning and reasoning with feedback, depending on whether agents adjust their plan in response to experimental or human feedback.

requirement: The system shall be robust and reliable by mitigating unreliable predictions such as hallucinated non-factual information, reasoning errors, systematic biases, and planning failures when connected with tools and experimental platforms.
exact_text_quote: is their propensity for generating unreliable predictions, including the hallucination of non-factual information, reasoning errors, systematic biases, and failures in planning when connected with tools and experimental platforms.

requirement: The system shall verify the agent action plan before execution to avoid stalling due to false negatives in embodied agents.
exact_text_quote: Hence, it is necessary to verify the agent action plan before execution.

requirement: The system shall provide transparent change logs and version control for agents to support reproducibility when agents integrate tools that may update without prior notice to users.
exact_text_quote: The scientific community needs transparent change logs and version control for agents, akin to practice in software development.

requirement: The system shall generate a sequence of function calls to a Semantic Scholar API to retrieve papers related to a given research topic.
exact_text_quote: we prompt an LLM to generate a sequence of function calls to the Semantic Scholar API.

requirement: The system shall score and rerank retrieved papers based on whether they are directly relevant to the specified topic, are empirical papers involving computational experiments, and are interesting enough to inspire new projects.
exact_text_quote: We then use the LLM to score and rerank all retrieved papers based on three criteria: 1) the paper should be directly relevant to the specified topic; 2) the paper should be an empirical paper involving computational experiments;333Note that we exclude position papers, survey papers, and analysis papers throughout this study since their evaluation tends to be very subjective. 3) the paper is interesting and can inspire new projects.

requirement: The system shall generate a large set of candidate seed ideas for each research topic.
exact_text_quote: Specifically, we prompt the LLM to generate 4000 seed ideas on each research topic.

requirement: The system shall remove duplicated ideas from the generated seed ideas by encoding ideas and computing pairwise cosine similarities with a similarity threshold of 0.8.
exact_text_quote: We set a similarity threshold of 0.8 for the idea deduplication based on manual inspection.

requirement: The system shall rank the remaining ideas using an automatic idea ranker built from public review data as a proxy.
exact_text_quote: To build such an automatic idea ranker, we use public review data as a proxy.

requirement: The system shall use a Swiss system tournament to obtain reliable scores for project proposals based on pairwise comparisons.
exact_text_quote: In order to obtain reliable scores for all project proposals based on pairwise comparisons, we adopt a Swiss system tournament where all project proposals are paired with those whose accumulated scores are similar, and if the proposals are judged to be better, they gain an additional point.

requirement: The system shall output the top-ranked project proposals from the agent for use as the AI ideas condition.
exact_text_quote: The top-ranked project proposals from the agent will be directly used for the AI Ideas condition of the human study.

requirement: The system shall provide an end-to-end code generation capability that includes loading datasets, implementing baseline methods, implementing the proposed method, loading or implementing evaluation metrics, and running experiments to produce a report of baseline and proposed method performance.
exact_text_quote: Specifically, we provide a template codebase that consists of: (1) loading datasets from Huggingface or generating synthetic test examples; (2) implementing baseline methods; (3) implementing the proposed method; (3) loading or implementing the evaluation metrics; (4) running experiments on the testset with the baselines and the proposed method, so that the output of the agent will be a report of the baseline performance as well as the proposed method’s performance.

requirement: The system shall enforce strict controls that standardize the styles of human and LLM ideas and match their topic distribution to reduce the possibility that confounding variables affect outcome measures.
exact_text_quote: To reduce the possibility that confounding variables affect our outcome measures, we enforce strict controls that standardize the styles of human and LLM ideas and match their topic distribution.

requirement: The system shall generate content or output such as text, images, audio, simulations, video, and codes from the data it is trained on.
exact_text_quote: GenAI can be defined as systems that are ‘designed to generate content or output (such as text, images, audio, simulations, video and codes) from the data they are trained on’ (Eke, [Citation2023](#), p. 1).

requirement: The system shall provide outputs that are coherent and well-formed text, images, and sound generated in new designs.
exact_text_quote: The output of GenAI is deemed to be ‘coherent and well-formed text, images, and sound generated in new designs’ (Tzirides et al., [Citation2023](#), p. 3).

requirement: The system shall support researchers by enabling the automation of certain tasks and augmenting their capabilities in data analysis, knowledge discovery, and problem-solving.
exact_text_quote: GenAI tools enable researchers to automate certain tasks, augment their capabilities, and explore new possibilities in data analysis, knowledge discovery, and problem-solving.

requirement: The system shall act as an analysis tool and a writing assistant for research reports.
exact_text_quote: They can also be used as an analysis tool as well as a writing assistant for research reports (Eke, [Citation2023](#)).

requirement: The system shall help researchers quickly and easily generate data samples for research based on patterns and structures learned from existing data.
exact_text_quote: For instance, GenAI can be a tool for the quick and easy generation of data samples for various types of research, based on patterns and structures learned from existing data (Eke, [Citation2023](#)).

requirement: The system shall provide training, support, and guidance to users to enable adequate and responsible use of GenAI tools.
exact_text_quote: However, participants emphasised the importance of adequate training, support, and guidance in using GenAI tools.

requirement: The system shall support responsible research practices by addressing ethical considerations such as transparency and potential biases associated with GenAI tools.
exact_text_quote: Ethical considerations emerged as a significant concern, highlighting the participants’ commitment to responsible research practices and the need for transparency and addressing potential biases associated with these tools.

requirement: The system shall ensure that users approach responses with caution and critical analysis due to potential inaccuracies or biases.
exact_text_quote: While users can ask questions, GPTs may provide relevant and convincing topics and responses, but their responses should be approached with caution and critical analysis due to potential inaccuracies or biases (Haleem et al., [Citation2022](#)).

requirement: The system shall mitigate risks related to the sources of information, the accuracy of purported facts, and the presence of explicit ethical frameworks.
exact_text_quote: Most importantly, GenAI systems pose potential risks to knowledge work, particularly in fundamental areas such as the sources of information, the accuracy of purported facts, the validity of underlying theories, the presence of explicit ethical frameworks, and the quality of critical dialogue (Tzirides et al., [Citation2023](#)).

requirement: The system shall address potential biases and inaccuracies that may be extended or amplified by the system.
exact_text_quote: These risks stem from the reliance on pre-existing data and the potential for biases and inaccuracies to be extended or amplified by these systems (Tzirides et al., [Citation2023](#)).

requirement: The system shall provide clear policies and procedures governing the use of GenAI tools, including guidelines for students and faculty members.
exact_text_quote: It is also crucial to establish clear policies and procedures governing their use, including guidelines for students and faculty members (Cotton et al., [Citation2023](#); Eke, [Citation2023](#)).

requirement: The system shall provide training and support to help users understand the ethical implications and responsible practices when utilising GenAI tools.
exact_text_quote: Providing training and support to users can help them understand the ethical implications and responsible practices when utilising these tools (Cotton et al., [Citation2023](#); Eke, [Citation2023](#)).

requirement: The system shall automatically define novel problems, propose methods, and design experiments.
exact_text_quote: This system automatically defines novel problems, proposes methods and designs experiments

requirement: The system shall iteratively refine generated research ideas based on feedback from collaborative LLM-powered reviewing agents.
exact_text_quote: while iteratively refining them based on the feedback from collaborative LLM-powered reviewing agents.

requirement: The system shall start from a core scientific paper and augment it with relevant publications by connecting information over an academic graph.
exact_text_quote: starting with a core scientific paper, ResearchAgent is augmented not only with relevant publications by connecting information over an academic graph

requirement: The system shall augment research idea generation with entities retrieved from a knowledge store derived from shared underlying concepts mined across numerous papers.
exact_text_quote: also entities retrieved from a knowledge store derived from shared underlying concepts mined across numerous papers.

requirement: The system shall use multiple LLM-based reviewing agents that provide reviews and feedback via iterative revision processes.
exact_text_quote: we leverage multiple LLM-based ReviewingAgents that provide reviews and feedback via iterative revision processes.

requirement: The system shall instantiate reviewing agents with human preference-aligned LLMs whose evaluation criteria are elicited from actual human judgments via LLM prompting.
exact_text_quote: These reviewing agents are instantiated with human preference-aligned LLMs whose criteria for evaluation are elicited from actual human judgments via LLM prompting.

requirement: The system shall support researchers by identifying knowledge gaps, proposing novel problem statements, and suggesting potential methodologies early in the research process.
exact_text_quote: In practice, it can support researchers by identifying knowledge gaps, proposing novel problem statements, and suggesting potential methodologies early in the research process.

requirement: The system shall assist in designing experiments and streamline the writing and refinement of research papers by generating drafts and offering feedback on how to effectively frame contributions and cite relevant work.
exact_text_quote: Also, it can assist in designing experiments and streamline the writing and refinement of research papers by generating drafts and offering feedback on how to effectively frame contributions and cite relevant work.

requirement: The system shall operationalize research idea generation by identifying problems, developing methods, and designing experiments using LLMs.
exact_text_quote: p =f (L) for identifying problems, m =f (p, L) for developing methods, and d = f (p, m, L) for designing experiments.

requirement: The system shall enhance idea generation by augmenting an LLM with relevant entities retrieved from its knowledge store.
exact_text_quote: we do this by augmenting the LLM with the relevant entities from K, which expand the context that LLMs consume with additional knowledge.

requirement: The system shall evaluate each generated research idea (problem, method, and experiment design) separately according to its own specific criteria using reviewing agents.
exact_text_quote: each of the generated research ideas (problem, method, and experiment design) is separately evaluated according to its own specific five criteria

requirement: The system shall align reviewing agents' evaluation criteria with actual scientific preferential standards by inducing criteria from real researchers' judgments.
exact_text_quote: Crucially, these ReviewingAgents are prompted with evaluation criteria that are induced from real researchers’ judgements, thus aligning them with actual scientific preferential standards.

requirement: The system shall generate human preference-aligned evaluation criteria for use in automatic evaluations.
exact_text_quote: we aim to ensure this by automatically generating human preference-aligned evaluation criteria (used for automatic evaluations)

requirement: The system shall provide a process to assess the trustworthiness of an AI system at any stage of its lifecycle.
exact_text_quote: It is designed to complement, rather than supplant, other assessments focused on the AI system’s compliance with relevant laws, standards, and regulations.

requirement: The system shall support a participatory process in which teams of experts assess ethical, technical, legal, and domain-specific implications of using an AI product or service within given contexts.
exact_text_quote: We created a *participatory process*, called Z-Inspection®, to help teams of skilled experts assess the *ethical*, *technical*, *legal*, and *domain-specific* implications of using an AI product or service within given contexts

requirement: The system shall include three phases: set-up, assess, and resolve.
exact_text_quote: The process comprises three phases: (1) set-up, (2) assess, and (3) resolve.

requirement: The system shall perform an assess phase that includes the creation and analysis of socio-technical scenarios and the identification of ethical issues and tensions.
exact_text_quote: The *assess phase* is an iterative process that includes the creation and analysis of socio-technical scenarios, the identification of ethical issues and tensions

requirement: The system shall validate claims by providing evidence during the assess phase when evidence is available.
exact_text_quote: the validation of claims by providing evidence (if any)

requirement: The system shall map identified issues to an ethical and trustworthy AI framework during the assess phase.
exact_text_quote: and the mapping to the EU trustworthy AI framework

requirement: The system shall address ethical tensions in the resolve phase by proposing trade-off solutions, identifying possible risks and remedies, and making recommendations to key stakeholders.
exact_text_quote: the *resolve phase* addresses the ethical tensions identified during the assess phase, here possible trade-off solutions are proposed, possible risks and remedies are identified, and recommendations are made to the key stakeholders.

requirement: The system shall support applying the process across the AI lifecycle including design, development, deployment, monitoring, and decommissioning.
exact_text_quote: The Z-Inspection® process can be applied to the entire AI lifecycle, typically including (1) design, (2) development, (3) deployment, (4) monitoring, and (5) decommissioning.

requirement: The system shall provide insight, adjustments, and recommendations on how to design a trustworthy system during the design phase.
exact_text_quote: In the *design phase*, the process can provide insight, adjustments, and recommendations on how to design a trustworthy system.

requirement: The system shall support specifying test cases during the development phase to verify the absence of certain biases.
exact_text_quote: During the *development phase*, the process can be used to specify test cases, e.g., to verify the absence of certain biases

requirement: The system shall support integrating the process into acceptance testing during deployment when trustworthiness is a specified user requirement.
exact_text_quote: During *deployment*, the process can be integrated into the acceptance test if trustworthiness is a specified user requirement.

requirement: The system shall support continuous monitoring of trustworthiness over time.
exact_text_quote: the trustworthiness of a system needs to be assessed as a continuous *monitoring* process.

requirement: The system shall keep protocols and logs over the full lifecycle to facilitate decommissioning and replacement.
exact_text_quote: Here, the protocols and logs (recording document) of the process over the full lifecycle of the old trustworthy AI system can facilitate the lifecycle of the new product.

requirement: The system shall use a holistic, interdisciplinary, and dynamic approach rather than monolithic and static ethical checklists.
exact_text_quote: Z-Inspection® uses a holistic, interdisciplinary, and dynamic approach, rather than monolithic and static ethical checklists.

requirement: The system shall tailor assessments to the use-case at hand.
exact_text_quote: In this way, the process has a certain degree of plasticity, which means that its assessments will be tailored to the use-case at hand.

requirement: The system shall expose and evaluate assumptions guiding the AI system’s creation and deployment as well as the assumptions of the researchers conducting the inspection.
exact_text_quote: It also means that the assumptions that guide the AI system’s creation and deployment—as well as the assumptions of the researchers conducting the Z-Inspection®—are exposed and evaluated during the inspection.

requirement: The system shall support an ongoing and iterative investigation of harms and benefits.
exact_text_quote: Finally, the dynamic lens reflects the commitment to an ongoing and iterative investigation of the harms and benefits of a particular AI system.

requirement: The system shall keep a log that tracks the process and events of the assessment.
exact_text_quote: In parallel to the phases, a log is kept in which the process and events of the assessment are tracked.

requirement: The system shall be a voluntary, non-binding assessment of potential ethical concerns and issues that may surface in the use of an AI system.
exact_text_quote: The Z-Inspection® process offers a voluntary, non-binding assessment of potential ethical concerns and issues that may surface in the use of an AI system.

requirement: The system shall enable LLM agent laboratories to upload and retrieve reports from a shared preprint server to collaborate, share insights, and iteratively build on each other’s research.
exact_text_quote: we introduce AgentRxiv—a framework that lets LLM agent laboratories upload and retrieve reports from a shared preprint server in order to collaborate, share insights, and iteratively build on each other’s research.

requirement: The system shall provide a centralized preprint server that enables agents to build upon the research of other agents.
exact_text_quote: Therefore, we aim to provide a unified platform that enables agents to build upon the research of other agents.

requirement: The system shall allow agents to cumulatively build on previous work by enabling systematic sharing of research findings.
exact_text_quote: By implementing a centralized, open-source preprint server for autonomous agents, AgentRxiv enables systematic sharing of research findings, allowing agents to cumulatively build on previous work.

requirement: The system shall support parallel research across multiple agentic systems to enable scalability with available computational resources.
exact_text_quote: AgentRxiv also supports parallel research across multiple agentic systems, enabling scalability with available computational resources.

requirement: The system shall provide routes for uploading, searching, and viewing papers and an API endpoint for returning search results in JSON format.
exact_text_quote: The web application provides routes for uploading, searching, and viewing papers, as well as an API endpoint for returning search results in JSON format.

requirement: The system shall extract text and basic metadata from a paper when it is uploaded by an agent.
exact_text_quote: When a paper is uploaded by an agent, the system extracts its text and basic metadata, and an update process synchronizes the database with the available files.

requirement: The system shall employ a similarity-based search mechanism for retrieval of papers.
exact_text_quote: For retrieval, AgentRxiv employs a similarity-based search mechanism.

requirement: The system shall compute text embeddings for both stored papers and incoming queries using a pre-trained SentenceTransformer model.
exact_text_quote: A pre-trained SentenceTransformer model is used to compute text embeddings for both the stored papers and incoming queries.

requirement: The system shall rank search results by cosine similarity between the query embedding and stored paper embeddings and return the top results.
exact_text_quote: When an agent submits a search query, the system calculates the cosine similarity between the query embedding and the embeddings of the stored papers, ranking the results based on their relevance and returning the top results.

requirement: The system shall allow multiple independent agent laboratories to run simultaneously and share findings through asynchronous access to papers produced by other laboratories.
exact_text_quote: Each autonomous laboratory performs literature review, experimentation, and report writing independently, while having asynchronous access to papers produced by other laboratories through AgentRxiv.

requirement: The system shall make a newly published research output immediately accessible to all other active laboratories for near real-time integration of findings.
exact_text_quote: Whenever one laboratory publishes a new research output to AgentRxiv, it becomes immediately accessible to all other active laboratories, enabling near real-time integration of findings.

requirement: The system shall provide a trade-off between speed and computational efficiency in its parallelized mode.
exact_text_quote: Although the parallelized mode accelerates improvements in wall-clock time, it introduces trade-offs between speed and computational efficiency.

requirement: The system shall support implementing generative AI systems by addressing hardware, software, and user experience requirements.
exact_text_quote: we present three distinct categories of requirements for AIGC: hardware, software, and user experience requirements.

requirement: The system shall provide a taxonomy of generative AI models based on architectural characteristics.
exact_text_quote: the study explores the different types of generative AI models described in the literature by presenting a taxonomy based on architectural characteristics

requirement: The system shall provide a comprehensive classification of input and output formats for generative AI tasks.
exact_text_quote: We provide a comprehensive classification of input and output formats for AIGC tasks, along with specific tasks and the corresponding models used in the literature, presented in a tabular format.

requirement: The system shall propose a classification system based on output types and discuss commonly used evaluation metrics for generative AI.
exact_text_quote: We propose a classification system based on the output types of generative AI and discuss commonly used evaluation metrics in the field.

requirement: The system shall facilitate effective implementation and evaluation of generative AI models for diverse applications.
exact_text_quote: enabling researchers, developers, and practitioners to effectively implement and evaluate generative AI models for various applications.

requirement: The system shall ensure user satisfaction by producing high-quality and realistic outputs.

requirement: The system shall support interactivity and responsiveness to user input.

requirement: The system shall support seamless integration with existing systems and compatibility with programming languages.

requirement: The system shall address ethical considerations including fairness and data privacy.
exact_text_quote: Interactivity and responsiveness to user input, along with ethical considerations, such as fairness and data privacy, are significant requirements.

requirement: The system shall use evaluation metrics tailored to the specific task or domain.
exact_text_quote: Evaluation metrics are tailored to the specific task or domain.

requirement: The system shall support generating multiple samples to explore the variety and creativity exhibited by the generative model.

exact_text_quote: Text to code enables us to generate entire source code for specific business problems, resolve issues within existing code by providing suggestions for corrections, while code to text empowers us to generate documentations for the code.

requirement: The system shall support code generation tasks that generate valid programming code using natural language descriptions.

requirement: The system shall evaluate code generation quality using metrics such as pass@metric.

requirement: The system shall develop code for Python and other computer languages.
exact_text_quote: (ix) develop code for Python and other computer languages.

requirement: The system shall help a writer break mental log jams when writing by providing initial text to start with.

requirement: The system shall identify references for a specific topic that might be missed by conventional literature searches.

requirement: The system shall ensure factual correctness of outputs because writing that does not adhere to the rigorous standards of scientific integrity should not be considered part of scientific knowledge.

requirement: The system shall support replicability by recording all interactions with the tools and the evaluation criteria.

requirement: The system shall enable users to program by using natural language as the programming language.

requirement: The system shall extract structured information from unstructured, natural-language inputs.

requirement: The system shall generate code for visualization tools from a natural-language description of the desired output.

requirement: The system shall be robust enough to handle prompt fragments leaking into output, using mechanisms such as retries with access to error messages or prompt engineering.
exact_text_quote: a challenge the sMolTalk tool faces is robustness. For instance, fragments from the prompt tend to leak into the output and must be handled with more involved mechanisms, such as retries (in which one gives the LLMs access to the error messages) or prompt engineering.

requirement: The system shall assist data scientists and natural scientists in generating code snippets or entire programs in response to user inquiries.
exact_text_quote: ChatGPT can furnish code snippets or even entire programs as responses to user inquiries.

requirement: The system shall help users debug programming errors by analyzing code and suggesting debugging strategies or code refinements.
exact_text_quote: The model can then assist in pinpointing potential culprits of the problem and suggest debugging strategies or code refinements to resolve it.

requirement: The system shall provide guidance for identifying and rectifying bugs in code through suggestions, explanations, and guidance.
exact_text_quote: This natural language processing model extends its support to programmers by furnishing suggestions, explanations, and guidance for identifying and rectifying bugs in their code.

requirement: The system shall support code understanding by providing explanations and descriptions of code snippets, functions, libraries, and frameworks in plain language.
exact_text_quote: ChatGPT significantly contributes to both endeavors by providing explanations and descriptions of code snippets, functions, libraries, and frameworks in plain language.

requirement: The system shall assist in generating documentation for code projects by analyzing the codebase and producing documentation that includes explanations, usage examples, and API references.
exact_text_quote: it can generate documentation encompassing explanations, usage examples, and API references.

requirement: The system shall support collaborative coding by acting as a virtual partner or collaborator that can co-author code.
exact_text_quote: Developers can employ ChatGPT as a virtual partner or collaborator, engaging in discussions about coding concepts, brainstorming solutions, and even co-authoring code.

requirement: The system shall require thorough testing and validation of its debugging solutions to ensure reliability and robustness.
exact_text_quote: However, the utilization of AI for debugging demands a rigorous regimen of testing and validation to ensure the reliability a nd robustness of the solutions provided.

requirement: The system shall be used as a supplementary resource rather than the sole solution for addressing intricate debugging challenges.

requirement: The system shall support agentic AI capabilities that can generate hypotheses, conduct literature reviews, design experiments, analyze data, and accelerate scientific discovery.
exact_text_quote: These systems, often referred to as agentic AI, can generate hypotheses, conduct literature reviews, design experiments, analyze data, accelerate scientific discovery, and reduce research costs [14], [15], [16], [17].

requirement: The system shall enable autonomous decision-making and multi-step reasoning by integrating large language models with modular toolkits.
exact_text_quote: we review AI-agent frameworks introduced between 2023 and 2025 that integrate large language models with modular toolkits to enable autonomous decision-making and multi-step reasoning.

requirement: The system shall provide code generation and software engineering capabilities for agentic AI tasks.

requirement: The system shall support retrieval-augmented generation by incorporating real-time data from knowledge bases, APIs, or the web.

requirement: The system shall dynamically orchestrate information retrieval and iterative refinement to manage complex workflows effectively.
exact_text_quote: Agentic RAG systems, which dynamically orchestrate information retrieval and iterative refinement to manage complex workflows effectively [12], [13].

requirement: The system shall support agentic workflows that actively formulate a strategy, carry out tasks using available tools, and evaluate outcomes.
exact_text_quote: an agent-based process involves AI agents who actively formulate a strategy, carry out tasks using available tools, and evaluate the outcomes.

requirement: The system shall provide a mechanism to decompose complex objectives into actionable steps during strategy development.
exact_text_quote: strategy development, essentially the planning phase, where the agent breaks down complex objectives into actionable steps.

requirement: The system shall support iterative thinking that enables the agent to reflect on its actions and refine its approach.
exact_text_quote: This is supported by an iterative thinking process, driven by reasoning and guided by prompts, which enables the agent to reflect on its ac- tions and refine its approach.

requirement: The system shall provide AI query engines and utility functions that interface with an integrated knowledge store to access static and real-time information.
exact_text_quote: Core operational support comes from AI query engines and utility functions that interface with an integrated knowledge store, ensuring that both static and real-time information is readily accessible.

requirement: The system shall ensure reliability and reproducibility of automated processes.
exact_text_quote: challenges persist, especially in executing domain-specific literature reviews and ensuring the reproducibility and reliability of automated pro- cesses [21], [22].

requirement: The system shall provide comprehensive evaluation frameworks that can reliably measure performance across various tasks.
exact_text_quote: underscoring the need for comprehensive evaluation frameworks that can reliably measure their performance across various healthcare tasks [32], [33], [34], [35].

requirement: The system shall ensure that human actors take final responsibility for scientific output when using generative AI-generated output in the research process.
exact_text_quote: we always need human actors to take on the final responsibility for scientific output.

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for interpretation of data analysis when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Interpretation of data analysis;

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for writing of manuscripts when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Writing of manuscripts;

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for evaluating manuscripts (journal editors) when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Evaluating manuscripts (journal editors);

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for peer review when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Peer review;

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for identifying research gaps when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Identifying research gaps;

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for formulating research aims when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Formulating research aims;

requirement: The system shall provide human verification for developing hypotheses when generative AI is used in research.
exact_text_quote: • Developing hypotheses.

requirement: The system shall support researchers in acknowledging and specifying which tasks they used generative AI for in scientific research publications or presentations.
exact_text_quote: Researchers should always acknowledge and specify for which tasks they have used generative AI in (scientific) research publications or presentations.

requirement: The system shall support researchers in acknowledging which generative AI tools (including which versions) they used in their work.
exact_text_quote: Researchers should acknowledge which generative AI tools (including which versions) they used in their work.

requirement: The system shall support preregistration of the use of generative AI in scientific research, including which prompts will be used, and making the input and output of generative AI tools available with the publication.
exact_text_quote: Researchers should preregister the use of generative AI in scientific research (such as which prompts they will use) and make the input and output of generative AI tools available with the publication.

requirement: The system shall recommend replication of findings with a different generative AI tool when a researcher has extensively used a generative AI tool in their work.
exact_text_quote: Researchers who have extensively used a generative AI tool in their work are recommended to replicate their findings with a different generative AI tool (if applicable).

requirement: The system shall support scientific journals in acknowledging their use of generative AI for peer review or selection purposes.
exact_text_quote: Scientific journals should acknowledge their use of generative AI for peer review or selection purposes.

requirement: The system shall support scientific journals in asking reviewers to what extent they used generative AI for their review.
exact_text_quote: Scientific journals should ask reviewers to what extent they used generative AI for their review.

requirement: The system shall enable generative AI developers and companies to make details of training data, training set-up, and algorithms for large language models fully available to an independent scientific organization before launching to society.
exact_text_quote: Generative AI developers and companies should make the details of the training data, training set-up and algorithms for large language models (LLMs) fully available to the independent scientific organization that facilitates the development of an auditing body (see ‘An auditor for generative AI’) before launching it to society.

requirement: The system shall enable generative AI developers and companies to share ongoing adaptations, training sets, and algorithms with the independent scientific auditing body.
exact_text_quote: Generative AI developers and companies should share ongoing adaptations, training sets and algorithms with the independent scientific auditing body.

requirement: The system shall provide a portal for users to report biased or inaccurate responses to the independent scientific auditing body and the company.
exact_text_quote: The independent scientific auditing body and generative AI companies should have a portal where users who discover biased or inaccurate responses can easily report them (the independent scientific auditing body should have access to this portal and actions taken by the company).

requirement: The system shall ensure that research funding organizations involve human assessment rather than relying completely on generative AI tools when evaluating research funding proposals.
exact_text_quote: Research funding organizations should not (completely) rely on generative AI tools in evaluating research funding proposals, but always involve human assessment.

requirement: The system shall support research funding organizations in acknowledging their use of generative AI tools for evaluating research proposals.
exact_text_quote: Research funding organizations should acknowledge their use of generative AI tools for evaluating research proposals.

requirement: The system shall maintain accountability by keeping humans in the loop to evaluate the quality of generated content.
exact_text_quote: Humans must remain in the loop to evaluate the quality of generated content;

requirement: The system shall not fully outsource crucial tasks such as writing manuscripts or peer reviews to generative AI.
exact_text_quote: we advocate that crucial tasks, such as writing manuscripts or peer reviews, should not be fully outsourced to generative AI.

requirement: The system shall support transparency by requiring researchers and other stakeholders to disclose their use of generative AI.
exact_text_quote: Researchers and other stakeholders should always disclose their use of generative AI.

requirement: The system shall provide independent oversight through external, objective auditing of generative AI tools.
exact_text_quote: External, objective auditing of generative AI tools is needed to ensure that they are of high quality and used ethically.

requirement: The system shall support continuous revision and adaptation of certification because the performance of generative AI systems evolves rapidly on the basis of user feedback and concerns.
exact_text_quote: Certification of generative AI systems requires continuous revision and adaptation, because the performance of these systems evolves rapidly on the basis of user feedback and concerns.

requirement: The system shall keep living guidelines up to date by having a second committee meet monthly to review the latest developments.
exact_text_quote: a second committee composed of about a dozen diverse scientific, policy and technical experts should meet monthly to review the latest developments.

requirement: The system shall ensure that the auditing body has sufficient computing power to run full-scale models.
exact_text_quote: It must have sufficient computing power to run full-scale models,

requirement: The system shall ensure that the auditing body has enough information about source codes to judge how they were trained.
exact_text_quote: and enough information about source codes to judge how they were trained.

requirement: The system shall support the auditing body in developing benchmarks that are updated regularly.
exact_text_quote: These benchmarks should be updated regularly.

requirement: The system shall support the auditing body in proactively seeking to prevent the introduction of harmful AI products while keeping policymakers, users and consumers informed of whether a product conforms to safety and effectiveness standards.
exact_text_quote: The auditing body should proactively seek to prevent the introduction of harmful AI products while keeping policymakers, users and consumers informed of whether a product conforms to safety and effectiveness standards.

requirement: The system shall support multi-step workflows that maintain a shared state across multiple model calls to provide context and consistency.
exact_text_quote: LLM-based agents, hereinafter also referred to as LLM agents, take the power of LLMs a step further by integrating them into a multi-step flow, while maintaining a state that is shared by multiple LLM calls, providing context and consistency.

requirement: The system shall utilize external tools to perform computations, access external knowledge, and interact with the environment.
exact_text_quote: They also utilize external tools to perform computations, access external knowledge and interact with their environment.

requirement: The system shall enable autonomous conception, execution, and adaptation of complex plans in real-world environments.
exact_text_quote: Agents are able to autonomously conceive, execute and adapt complex plans in real-world environments.

requirement: The system shall provide evaluation support for fundamental agent capabilities including planning and multi-step reasoning, tool use, self-reflection, and memory.
exact_text_quote: These include planning and multi-step reasoning, tool use, self-reflection, and memory.

requirement: The system shall support function calling to interact with external tools through function calling.
exact_text_quote: The ability of LLMs to interact with external tools through function calling is fundamental for building intelligent agents capable of delivering real-time, contextually accurate responses

requirement: The system shall support the integrated sub-process of function calling that includes intent recognition, function selection, parameter-value-pair mapping, function execution, and response generation.
exact_text_quote: Intent recognition identifies when a function is needed based on user requests. Function selection determines the most appropriate tool for the task. Parameter-value-pair mapping extracts relevant arguments from the conversation and assigns them to function parameters. Function execution invokes the selected function with those parameters to interact with external systems. Finally, response generation processes the function output and incorporates it into the LLM’s reply to the user.

requirement: The system shall support stepwise evaluation of individual agent actions or LLM calls to facilitate root cause analysis of errors.
exact_text_quote: Most evaluation frameworks support granular assessments of individual agent actions or LLM calls, facilitating root cause analysis of errors.

requirement: The system shall support trajectory-based assessment by analyzing the sequence of steps taken by an agent in relation to an expected optimal path.
exact_text_quote: some platforms—such as Google Vertex AI (Google Cloud, [2025](https://arxiv.org/html/2503.16416v1#bib.bib38)) and LangSmith (LangChain, [2023](https://arxiv.org/html/2503.16416v1#bib.bib64)) support trajectory-based assessments, which analyze the sequence of steps taken by an agent in relation to an expected optimal path.

requirement: The system shall support continuous monitoring of agent trajectories and assessment of key performance metrics such as task completion rates, latency, and execution speed.
exact_text_quote: All evaluation platforms provide continuous monitoring of agent trajectories, assessing key performance metrics such as task completion rates, latency, execution speed

requirement: The system shall support evaluation in controlled, interactive settings using simulated environments.
exact_text_quote: evaluation often requires a controlled, interactive setting with a simulated environment, provided by Gym-like frameworks.

requirement: The system shall support realistic and challenging evaluations that more accurately reflect real-world complexities.
exact_text_quote: there is a clear shift toward benchmarks that more accurately reflect real-world complexities.

requirement: The system shall support continuously updated evaluation methodologies to remain adaptive as models improve.
exact_text_quote: the rapid pace of LLM and agent development necessitates evaluation methodologies that are adaptive and continuously updated.

requirement: The system shall integrate cost efficiency as a core metric by tracking factors such as token usage, API expenses, inference time, and overall resource consumption.
exact_text_quote: Future evaluation frameworks should integrate cost efficiency as a core metric, tracking factors such as token usage, API expenses, inference time, and overall resource consumption.
